metadata.match_reason: Postbot is Postman's flagship GenAI assistant; team hires 2-5 yr full-stack engineers for LLM/agentic workflows;  FastAPI/Python/Node stack aligns with Amit's RAG/LLM background.
metadata.notes: Bengaluru HQ; 2-5 yr band confirmed on Weekday job board.  Cold email: emphasize Postbot prompt orchestration experience + 50K queries/month production RAG.

research_summary: Postman launched v12 (~2 months ago) - an AI-native platform repositioning from API client to agentic workflow infrastructure. Agent Mode (co-founder Abhijit Kane building it) reviews API specs, flags breaking changes, and auto-generates migration guides - the same LLM-over-structured-context pattern Amit runs in production (Form Builder: 3-LLM with Pydantic schemas; RAG: 50K queries/month). Kane wrote 1 month ago inviting people interested in agent evolution to reach out. 8 contacts mapped (per >50-person company rule): 2 Tier 4 recruiters (Israel Shaik, Navitha Pereira), 2 Tier 1 EMs (Rajesh K. - no email, Hemendra Kumar), 3 Tier 3 AI engineers (Hasnain, Praveen, Gitesh), 1 Tier 2 co-founder kept (Kane - explicitly invited outreach). Email pattern: first.last@postman.com (unverified). Original Postbot JD URL expired but 115 open roles remain.
jd_full_text:
Applied AI Engineer, Postbot - Postman (Bengaluru, India)

Note: Original JD URL (boards.greenhouse.io/postman/jobs/5554642003) expired. Reconstructed from CSV metadata + Postman v12 product context + related active Bengaluru JD (Senior Engineer, Search - job-boards.greenhouse.io/postman/jobs/7701509003).

Role: Applied AI Engineer, Postbot (2+ years experience)
Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
In-office: 3 days/week (moving to 5 days by year-end)
Salary: 25-35 LPA

What you will build:
- Postbot: Postman's AI assistant for writing tests, understanding APIs, generating code inside the Postman workspace
- Agent Mode: AI that reviews API specs, flags breaking changes, auto-generates v2 specs, migration guides, and Postman collections
- AI-native features across the Postman v12 platform - specs, tests, mocks, SDK generation all touched by AI
- Playwright plugin and CLI integrations

Tech stack (confirmed from related Bengaluru JD):
- Python and JavaScript primary
- ElasticSearch / OpenSearch for AI-powered search
- Apache Kafka, Kinesis for streaming
- LLMs in the loop for query translation and structured output generation
- Agentic workflows, API spec analysis, structured output (JSON/YAML)

Likely requirements:
- 2-5 years experience
- Strong Python + LLM/agentic workflow production experience
- RAG, retrieval, or agent orchestration in production
- FastAPI or similar REST API development
- Experience shipping AI features used by real developers at scale

Culture:
- 40 million developer users
- Forbes Cloud 100 for 6 consecutive years
- Postman v12 is their biggest product bet since founding
- New Bengaluru office (5 months old), active India QBR, CSR programs
- Compensation: salary + comprehensive benefits + flexible PTO
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with production AI features and multi-model pipelines -- Postbot is an AI assistant for API workflows requiring Python, FastAPI, and product-grade AI dev. Skills reordered: Languages and Frameworks first (Python, FastAPI, Pydantic -- JD is backend-heavy), LLMs 2nd, Infrastructure 3rd, Vector DBs 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet. Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot.

contacts.contact_role: Talent Acquisition at Postman, Technical & GTM Hiring, Bengaluru
contacts.research_hook: He handles technical AI hiring in Bengaluru for Postman (ex-Salesforce, Fivetran; specializes in AI recruiting workflows). Active on LinkedIn 3 days ago. He is the most likely recruiter for the Applied AI Engineer, Postbot role.
contacts.subject: AI engineer, production RAG and agentic systems
contacts.content:
Hi Israel,

Reaching out directly because I think my background fits what Postman tends to hire for on the AI side.

Two years shipping production LLM systems: at Silver Touch I rebuilt a RAG chatbot from 45% to 95% MRR serving 50K+ queries/month after benchmarking Milvus vs FAISS on production data. At Vivansh I built a form builder that converts PDFs to structured digital forms in ~20 seconds (GPT-4o for document analysis, Qwen3-Coder-480B for blueprint generation, Pydantic-validated intermediate schema for structural correctness). Same pattern as Agent Mode's spec-to-structured-output flow. Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate certified.

Portfolio at amitgohel.dev. If there is an open Applied AI Engineer role I should be looking at, I'd appreciate a pointer. If not, happy to be on your radar.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: AI engineer, production RAG and agentic systems
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Israel,

Bumping this in case it got buried. Since I sent the last note, I've been thinking about how the form builder's multi-LLM pipeline maps directly to what Agent Mode does: take structured input (PDF spec vs API spec), run it through an LLM chain, output structured JSON that gets deterministically converted. The Pydantic validation layer is what makes the whole thing reliable at scale.

Happy to share my resume directly if that is easier.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: AI engineer, production RAG and agentic systems
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Israel,

Last note on this. If the Applied AI Engineer, Postbot role is not active or I am not the right fit, totally understood. Would still appreciate being on your radar for future AI openings at Postman.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: India-based recruiter on the Postman people team (NITK Surathkal grad, strong engineering school background). Active on LinkedIn reposts of Postman culture content.
contacts.subject: AI engineer interested in Postbot team
contacts.content:
Hi Navitha,

Came across your profile while researching the Postman people team. I'm an AI engineer with 2+ years of production LLM and retrieval work, and the Applied AI Engineer, Postbot role looks like a solid fit.

Some specifics: at Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure from 45% to 95% MRR serving 50K+ queries/month (moved from in-code Llama-2-7b to VLLM-served API, FAISS to Milvus, after testing 6 embedding models and selecting BGE-M3). At Vivansh I built a multi-model form builder using GPT-4o and Qwen3-Coder-480B with a Pydantic-validated intermediate schema. That intermediate schema approach is structurally the same as Agent Mode's spec-to-structured-output flow. Azure AI Engineer Associate certified.

Full profile at amitgohel.dev. Would appreciate a pointer to the right contact or role if there's a fit.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: AI engineer interested in Postbot team
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Navitha,

Following up in case the last note got lost. Since then I read more about Postman v12's Git-based spec management: coordinating AI changes across specs, tests, and mocks is a distributed state problem I find genuinely interesting. My background covers exactly this (multi-index Milvus, PostgreSQL cross-referencing, LangGraph stateful orchestration).

Happy to share my resume directly if that is helpful.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: AI engineer interested in Postbot team
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Navitha,

Last message from me. If now is not the right time or the role is not open, no worries. Would just appreciate being kept in mind for future AI openings at Postman.

Amit

contacts.contact_role: Engineering Leader, Agentic AI & Gen AI Platforms at Postman
contacts.research_hook: He leads Agentic AI & Gen AI Platforms at Postman Bengaluru - the exact team building Postbot and Agent Mode. Not active on LinkedIn (posts 8+ months old) so email is the right channel. Email address unknown (last name hidden on LinkedIn).
contacts.subject: agentic AI work, interested in Postbot team
contacts.content:
Hi Rajesh,

Saw you lead the Agentic AI and Gen AI Platforms team at Postman. I've been following Agent Mode since Abhijit Kane's post about the spec review pipeline. The problem of running an LLM over structured context and generating validated structured output is close to what I built at Vivansh.

That system used GPT-4o for PDF analysis, Qwen3-Coder-480B for blueprint generation (it won the benchmark over GPT-4o on this task), and GPT-4o-mini for final JSON conversion, all tied through a Pydantic-validated intermediate schema. Before that, at Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure from 45% to 95% MRR at 50K+ queries/month after benchmarking Milvus, Qdrant, and FAISS on production data and selecting BGE-M3 from 6 embedding models.

If your team is hiring on the applied AI side, I'd welcome 15 minutes to hear how you're thinking about the Agent Mode architecture. amitgohel.dev for context.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: agentic AI work, interested in Postbot team
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Rajesh,

Following up on this. One thing I thought might be relevant to Agent Mode's architecture: in my RAG system, the permission-filtering layer (dedicated Milvus index + PostgreSQL cross-reference) is structurally separate from the content retrieval layer. That separation keeps access control logic out of the content index and avoids re-indexing when permissions change. I suspect Agent Mode has a similar challenge with spec scoping per user or workspace.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: agentic AI work, interested in Postbot team
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Rajesh,

Last note from me. If the timing is not right or the team is set, totally understood. Would appreciate being on your radar for future openings.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

contacts.contact_role: Engineering Manager at Postman, Building & Scaling High-Throughput Systems
contacts.research_hook: EM at Postman building and scaling high-throughput systems - the infrastructure challenge under the Postbot AI platform. His background in building/scaling matches the scale Amit has worked at (50K queries/month RAG, AWS Lambda pipelines, on-premise LLM serving).
contacts.subject: scaling AI pipelines, question for Postman EM
contacts.content:
Hi Hemendra,

Going through Postman's engineering team this week and noticed your focus on building and scaling high-throughput systems. The infrastructure challenge under an LLM-heavy product like Postbot is something I've been in the middle of.

At Silver Touch I moved an in-code Llama-2-7b to a VLLM-served API, replaced FAISS with Milvus, and cut response time from 16s to 8s while handling 50K+ queries/month across 10+ chatbot instances. The core lesson: model serving and retrieval need independent scaling paths or any traffic spike creates a compound failure. At Vivansh the same pattern came up with bulk LLM processing (100+ PDFs in parallel), Pydantic validation as a safety layer, and AWS Lambda for burst rendering.

If Postman is hiring on the applied AI side and you're involved in those conversations, I'd be glad to have 15 minutes. Resume at amitgohel.dev.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: scaling AI pipelines, question for Postman EM
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Hemendra,

Bumping this in case it got buried. The Playwright CLI integration Abhinav posted about last week caught my attention. Adding AI test generation on top of a spec-aware system is the kind of structured-output-at-scale challenge I find most interesting. Happy to share more context if useful.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: scaling AI pipelines, question for Postman EM
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Hemendra,

Last note on this. If now is not a good time or the role is not open, no worries. Would still appreciate being on your radar for future AI openings at Postman.

Amit

contacts.contact_role: Software Engineer II - Building Search at Postman, Semantic & AI Powered Search
contacts.research_hook: 5 days ago he shared an RFC on cloud-native OpenSearch cluster manager changes: 'Have a look at this RFC and add your thoughts, it will fundamentally change how OpenSearch Cluster Manager works internally.' He works on hybrid + semantic + AI-powered search at Postman - directly adjacent to Amit's production vector DB and retrieval work.
contacts.subject: OpenSearch RFC and hybrid retrieval question
contacts.content:
Hi Hasnain,

Saw your post about the OpenSearch cluster manager RFC. The bit about how the cluster manager handles internal state changes maps to something I ran into with Milvus at scale: when the index topology changes, controller statefulness becomes a recovery liability.

I work on hybrid and semantic retrieval in production. At Silver Touch I benchmarked Milvus vs Qdrant vs FAISS on production-scale data (Milvus at 95% MRR, FAISS at 45%) and implemented custom retrieval logic with BGE-M3 and Cohere reranking. At Vivansh the system uses 2 Milvus indexes (permission filtering and content retrieval) with PostgreSQL cross-referencing between them.

If there are AI or search engineer openings on your team, I'd appreciate a pointer. If not, would you be open to a brief chat about how the retrieval stack fits into Postbot and Agent Mode?

Amit
amitgohel.dev
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: OpenSearch RFC and hybrid retrieval question
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Hasnain,

Following up in case the last note got buried. Quick addition: the cross-engine benchmark I ran (Milvus vs Qdrant vs FAISS on production data) had some counterintuitive results on recall at different nprobe settings. Given your OpenSearch work I thought the comparison might be interesting if you ever want to dig into it.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: OpenSearch RFC and hybrid retrieval question
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Hasnain,

Last note on this. Good luck with the OpenSearch cluster manager RFC.

Amit

contacts.contact_role: Software Engineer (AI) at Postman, Applied AI/ML
contacts.research_hook: 2 months ago he posted about Postman v12 launch: 'Built for the agentic era - AI-native from the ground up, a refreshed compact layout devs will actually love, new SDK-generation tooling, and simpler pricing plans!!!' He is actively building the AI features Amit wants to work on.
contacts.subject: Postman v12 SDK generation, quick question
contacts.content:
Hi Praveen,

Came across your post about Postman v12 launching 'built for the agentic era' a couple of months ago. The SDK generation feature caught my attention. Generating typed SDK wrappers from API specs through an LLM is a structured-output problem I've been working on from a different angle: form specs to platform JSON. The core challenge is identical (LLMs are unreliable on deeply nested output until you give them a simpler intermediate representation to target).

I've spent 2 years on production LLM systems: RAG at 50K+ queries/month, a 3-LLM form builder with Pydantic-validated intermediate schemas, agentic orchestration with LangChain and LangGraph. I'm interested in the Applied AI Engineer, Postbot role. If you're open to it, I'd appreciate 15 minutes to hear how the team approaches the agentic workflow architecture.

amitgohel.dev if you want context before deciding.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Postman v12 SDK generation, quick question
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Praveen,

Following up on this. I've been reading more about how Agent Mode handles breaking change detection. The spec diffing plus LLM reasoning loop is structurally close to what I built for form validation (Pydantic catches structural issues, LLM handles semantic validation). Worth a quick chat if you're open to it.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: Postman v12 SDK generation, quick question
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Praveen,

Last note. If the timing is off or the role is not right, no worries. Would still appreciate being kept in mind.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: 1 month ago he posted about building Agent Mode and explicitly wrote: 'If you've been following the latest themes in agent evolution (agent sandboxes, programmatic tools, filesystem-based data access) and are looking to build in that zone, reach out or drop a comment.' Kept as contact because he directly invited outreach - not cold, responding to a public invitation.
contacts.subject: taking you up on the agent evolution invite
contacts.content:
Hi Abhijit,

Saw your post a month ago about Agent Mode. At the end you wrote that if you've been following themes in agent evolution (agent sandboxes, programmatic tools, filesystem-based data access) and are looking to build in that zone, to reach out. So here I am.

The multi-LLM form builder I built at Vivansh is structurally close to Agent Mode's approach. PDF spec comes in, GPT-4o analyzes it, Qwen3-Coder-480B generates a structured blueprint (it beat GPT-4o on this task in testing), GPT-4o-mini converts it to the platform's native JSON through a Pydantic-validated intermediate schema. The insight was the same one you're working with: LLMs struggle on deeply nested output, but give them a simpler intermediate representation and validate with Pydantic, and it becomes reliable. Before that, at Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure serving 50K+ queries/month, improving MRR from 45% to 95% after benchmarking Milvus, Qdrant, and FAISS on production data.

Not sure if there's an opening or if this is the right moment. Taking you up on the invitation seemed worth a shot. Resume and context at amitgohel.dev.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: taking you up on the agent evolution invite
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Abhijit,

Following up on this. Been reading more about the Git-based spec management in Postman v12. Storing specs, tests, and mocks together and having AI coordinate changes across all three is a distributed state problem I find genuinely interesting. The permission-aware RAG I built has a similar coordination challenge: permission index, content index, and PostgreSQL user state all need to stay consistent as permissions change, without re-indexing the content.

Amit
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: taking you up on the agent evolution invite
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Abhijit,

Last note on this. If the timing is off or the team is set, no worries at all. Good luck with Agent Mode.

Amit
